content.problemStatement:
Write a program to search for a specific element in an array and print "**Yes**" if the element is present, otherwise print "**No**".


Input Format

The first line contains an integer nnn, the length of the array and kkk, the element to be search.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Print "**Yes**" if the element kkk is present in the array.
Print "**No**" if the element kkk is not present in the array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers n and k, where n is the length of the array and k is the element to search for; the second line contains n space‑separated integers representing the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if k is present in the array, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The array contains the element 1, so the answer is Yes.
The element 4 does not appear in the array, therefore the output is No.
The element 5 is present at the last position, so we print Yes.

solution.approach: For each test case we read n and k, then scan the n array elements one by one. As soon as an element equal to k is found we can stop scanning (optional) and mark the test case as successful. After processing the whole array we output "Yes" if the marker is set, otherwise "No". This is a straightforward linear search requiring O(n) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, total O(T·n) where n is the size of each individual array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input storage.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int n,k; scanf("%d %d",&n,&k); int found=0; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int x; scanf("%d",&x); if(x==k) found=1;} printf("%s\n", found?"Yes":"No");} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=list(map(int,sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    idx=0
    T=data[idx]; idx+=1
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        n=data[idx]; k=data[idx+1]; idx+=2
        arr=data[idx:idx+n]; idx+=n
        out.append('Yes' if k in arr else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
